description: Pre-CI AI authoring checklist for HorrorPlace-Atrocity-Seeds. Ensures seeds, docs, and scripts are schema-bound, implication-only, and correctly placed in the VM-constellation.

global_rules.description: Each authoring request must propose exactly one primary file plus optional registry edits. No multi-file dumps.
global_rules.ai_action: If the user asks for multiple artifacts, negotiate a sequence; do not return multiple unrelated files at once.

global_rules.description: Never embed raw horror content, explicit violence, or narrative description of harm in any Atrocity-Seeds artifact. Seeds and docs must remain implication-first.
global_rules.ai_action: Use IDs, hashes, invariant/metric bindings, and style references only. Replace any narrative idea with references to upstream archives and style contracts.

global_rules.description: Atrocity-Seeds is a sealed vault. Engines and runtime systems MUST NOT query this repo directly in production.
global_rules.ai_action: When generating scripts or docs, always route runtime access through Horror.Place registries and Orchestrator descriptors, not direct git clone/HTTP fetch of Atrocity-Seeds in live builds.

high_intensity_rule.ai_action: If intensityband >= 8, you MUST include a deadledgerref compatible with Dead-Ledger zkpproof and verifier conventions.

ai_actions: Never invent new top-level fields. Conform exactly to eventcontract_v1.json / regioncontract_v1.json.
If the user describes story content, transform it into invariant and metrics bindings, not text fields.
Always set schemaref to the canonical Horror.Place schema URI.

path_rule.description: Registry entries must point to Atrocity-Seeds via git@ URIs with fragment paths.

invariant_bundle_rule.description: Must reference a Black-Archivum invariant bundle by git@ URI.

ai_actions: Never hard-code HTTP URLs or raw paths; always use git@ URIs.
When adding a new seed, update the relevant registry only after the seed JSON is fully valid.

constraints: Do not expand ranges of invariants or metrics beyond the canonical definitions.
Do not set additionalProperties: true on invariants or metrics blocks.
ai_actions: Treat Atrocity-Seeds schemas as strict mirrors of Horror.Place; add only annotations or repo-local metadata, not structural changes.
If implementing seed generation, ensure generated files conform to this checklist and canonical schemas.
Never embed API keys, private keys, or direct database connections.

must_state: Atrocity-Seeds is a Tier-2 PCG seed vault for invariant-bound horror, implication-only.
Engines must never query this vault at runtime in production.
All seeds are validated against Horror.Place schemas and hashed before signing and dispatch.
High-intensity seeds require Dead-Ledger attestation via deadledgerref.
forbidden: Explicit descriptions of violence or suffering
ai_actions: Use architectural, schema, and pipeline language in docs.
Refer to history and trauma only via invariant bundles and IDs.
